methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, is valued at $2.5B post-money following $850M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private-sector participants in space and aerospace, including Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $2.5B last post-money valuation, implying $4.2B at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage private comparables—Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.3B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. Public-market anchoring via P/S multiples (median 64.2x across the sector) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures.

Triangulation of these two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $4.9B, with a median of $2.8B. The current $2.5B valuation sits within this range but above the peer median, reflecting either a premium relative to comparable private financings or potential divergence in growth trajectory or market positioning. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, technology differentiation within the addressable space-infrastructure market, and execution risk on near-term milestones.
